contentBlocks.title: When do you need to convert tonnes to pounds?
contentBlocks.html:
Metric tonne (t) equals 1,000 kg. Pound (lb) is the standard mass unit in the US imperial system. 1 tonne = 2,204.62 pounds.

This conversion is essential for international shipping, freight logistics, agriculture (crop yields), and heavy industry where weight limits differ between metric and imperial systems.

Examples: 1 t = 2,205 lbs, 5 t = 11,023 lbs, 20 t = 44,092 lbs. A typical car weighs about 1.5 t = 3,307 lbs.

All calculations run locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.

contentBlocks.title: How does tonnes to pounds conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
1 metric tonne = 1,000 kg. 1 kg = 2.20462 lbs. Therefore, 1 tonne = 2,204.62 lbs.

Note: the US short ton = 2,000 lbs = 907.185 kg. The UK long ton = 2,240 lbs = 1,016.05 kg. This converter uses the metric tonne (1,000 kg).

To convert tonnes to pounds, multiply by 2,204.62. To convert pounds to tonnes, divide by 2,204.62.

contentBlocks.title: Practical tips
contentBlocks.html:
• Car: 1.2–2.0 t = 2,646–4,409 lbs. SUV: 2.0–2.5 t = 4,409–5,512 lbs.
  • Shipping container (20ft, loaded): up to 21.6 t = 47,620 lbs.
  • US short ton = 2,000 lbs = 907 kg (not a metric tonne!).
  • 1 metric tonne of water = 1,000 liters = 264 US gallons.

items.question: What is the difference between a tonne and a ton?
items.answer: Metric tonne = 1,000 kg = 2,205 lbs. US short ton = 2,000 lbs = 907 kg. UK long ton = 2,240 lbs = 1,016 kg.

items.question: How heavy is a car in tonnes?
items.answer: Compact car: 1.0–1.3 t. Sedan: 1.3–1.8 t. SUV: 1.8–2.5 t. Pickup truck: 2.0–3.0 t.

items.question: Are calculations done locally?
items.answer: Yes. All calculations run in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
